data.content:
Here are the two incremental build issues found in `IncrementalBroken.csproj`:

### 1. `GenerateTimestamp` (line 6–11)
Writes `$([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())` to a file before every compile. The value changes every second, so the output file always changes, forcing `CoreCompile` to re-run. It also lacks `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes, so MSBuild never skips it.

### 2. `EmbedGitHash` (line 13–23)
Runs `git rev-parse HEAD` and writes a `.cs` file **every build**. Even when the hash hasn't changed, `Overwrite="true"` updates the file timestamp, making `CoreCompile` see a changed input. It also lacks `Inputs`/`Outputs`, so the target itself always executes.

---

**Fixes needed:**

| Target | Problem | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `GenerateTimestamp` | Embeds ever-changing `DateTime.Now` | Remove or make the timestamp deterministic (e.g. use a reproducible/fixed value), and add `Inputs`/`Outputs` |
| `EmbedGitHash` | Always overwrites file even when content is unchanged | Read existing file first, only write when content differs; add `Inputs`/`Outputs` so the target can be skipped |

Would you like me to apply these fixes?
data.reasoningText:
Now let me analyze the incremental build issues in this project.

There are two custom targets:

1. **`GenerateTimestamp`** (line 6-11): This runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile"` and writes the current timestamp to a file. Since `$([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())` changes every time, this will always produce a different file, which means `CoreCompile` will always detect a changed input and re-run. Additionally, this target has no `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes, so MSBuild will always run it.

2. **`EmbedGitHash`** (line 13-23): This runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile"` and executes `git rev-parse HEAD` then writes the result to a generated `.cs` file. 

The problem is that both targets lack `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes, so they always execute regardless of whether anything actually changed. Even worse, the `EmbedGitHash` target uses `WriteLinesToFile` with `Overwrite="true"`, which updates the file's timestamp even when the git hash hasn't changed—tricking CoreCompile into thinking its inputs have been modified. This completely breaks incremental builds.

I need to fix these by adding proper `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes and ensuring files only get rewritten when their content actually changes.
